title: incomplete display of the input password dialog when startup
author: red-stream
body_snippet:
I've set "passwd protect" to yes. when reopen the software I got a incomplete display of the input password dialog. although i still can input my passwd to use it normally, but it remains a bug.
**mRemoteNG version 1.76.20.24615**

## Expected Behavior
display like the normal

## Current Behavior
It seems the **OK** and **Cancel** button can not be show out because of his too short and narrow parent box.

## Possible Solution
may be to consider of a particular Monitor Resolution

## ...

iterations.description: The password dialog's height is incorrectly calculated due to a manual sizing adjustment that doesn't account for dynamic content changes, leading to an incomplete display.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The password dialog's height is incorrectly calculated due to a manual sizing adjustment that doesn't account for dynamic content changes, leading to an incomplete display.
Approach: Remove manual form height adjustment in code and enable AutoSize and AutoSizeMode.GrowAndShrink on the form to allow dynamic resizing based on content.
